content.query:
/* ============================================================================
   ER_Juror ETL  --  Audit & Reporting setup
   Run order:
     1) Section A  -- once, in a Synapse PySpark notebook (creates Delta tables)
     2) Section B  -- once, in Synapse Serverless SQL (creates the two reports)
     3) Section C  -- ad-hoc, query the views from Power BI / SSMS / Synapse
   ============================================================================ */


/* ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   SECTION A  --  Delta audit tables (run from a PySpark notebook cell)
   ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Why Delta and not a SQL pool table?
     - L0/L1/L2/L3 already write to Delta in your lake. Keeping the audit data
       in Delta means a single, append-only source of truth that Spark notebooks
       can write to cheaply, and that Synapse Serverless can read with OPENROWSET.
     - No CETAS, no copies, no second storage layer.

   Replace <storage> with your storage account name, e.g. erjurorstgaccount.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

-- (run as %%sql in a PySpark notebook, or wrapped in spark.sql(...) )

CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS audit;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit.pipeline_run_metrics (
    run_id            STRING    NOT NULL,
    date_ingested     DATE      NOT NULL,
    la_code           STRING,                    -- e.g. '169-Liverpool', null for global rows
    stage             STRING    NOT NULL,        -- 'L0' | 'L1' | 'L2' | 'POSTGRES' | 'L3'
    row_count         BIGINT    NOT NULL,
    deduplicated_rows BIGINT,                    -- only populated for L2 / L3
    notes             STRING,                    -- 'Empty fname cnt 25, address UNKNOWN 200' etc.
    process_name      STRING,                    -- pipeline / notebook name
    captured_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
)
USING DELTA
LOCATION 'abfss://audit@<storage>.dfs.core.windows.net/pipeline_run_metrics'
PARTITIONED BY (date_ingested);


CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit.pipeline_file_errors (
    run_id        STRING    NOT NULL,
    date_ingested DATE      NOT NULL,
    la_code       STRING,                        -- null when LA cannot be derived
    file_name     STRING,
    file_format   STRING,                        -- 'CSV' | 'Excel' | 'ZIP' | ''
    file_location STRING,                        -- abfss path
    error_code    STRING    NOT NULL,            -- internal code (file_unreadable etc.)
    error_message STRING    NOT NULL,            -- human readable, matches your report
    comments      STRING,                        -- 'incorrect format', 'cant open the file', ...
    captured_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
)
USING DELTA
LOCATION 'abfss://audit@<storage>.dfs.core.windows.net/pipeline_file_errors'
PARTITIONED BY (date_ingested);


/* ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   SECTION B  --  Synapse Serverless SQL views over the Delta tables
   ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   These produce exactly the two report shapes you asked for.
   Run in Synapse Studio  ->  Develop  ->  SQL script  ->  connect to Built-in
   (Serverless SQL pool).
---------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

-- Create a database in serverless to hold the views (one-time)
IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE name = N'er_juror_audit')
    CREATE DATABASE er_juror_audit;
GO
USE er_juror_audit;
GO

-- Credential / data source: re-use what you already have for the lake.
-- If you have not got one yet, an example pattern (commented) is below.
/*
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE [DataLakeAudit]
    WITH (LOCATION = 'abfss://audit@<storage>.dfs.core.windows.net');
*/

-- ---------- Report 1: Row counts and discrepancies -------------------------
CREATE OR ALTER VIEW dbo.vw_pipeline_row_counts
AS
WITH metrics AS (
    SELECT
        run_id,
        date_ingested,
        la_code,
        stage,
        row_count,
        deduplicated_rows,
        notes
    FROM OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'pipeline_run_metrics/',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'DataLakeAudit',
        FORMAT = 'DELTA'
    ) WITH (
        run_id            VARCHAR(64),
        date_ingested     DATE,
        la_code           VARCHAR(128),
        stage             VARCHAR(16),
        row_count         BIGINT,
        deduplicated_rows BIGINT,
        notes             VARCHAR(2000)
    ) AS m
)
SELECT
    CONVERT(CHAR(8), m.date_ingested, 112)                                          AS [Date Ingested],
    m.la_code                                                                       AS [LA],
    MAX(CASE WHEN m.stage = 'L0'       THEN m.row_count END)                        AS [L0 Rows Cnt],
    MAX(CASE WHEN m.stage = 'L1'       THEN m.row_count END)                        AS [L1 Rows Cnt],
    MAX(CASE WHEN m.stage = 'L2'       THEN m.row_count END)                        AS [L2 Rows Cnt],
    MAX(CASE WHEN m.stage = 'POSTGRES' THEN m.row_count END)                        AS [Postgresql Rows Cnt],
    MAX(CASE WHEN m.stage = 'L3'       THEN m.row_count END)                        AS [L3 Rows Cnt],
    SUM(CASE WHEN m.stage IN ('L2','L3') THEN m.deduplicated_rows END)              AS [Deduplicated Rows],
    -- Concatenate every non-null note, ordered by stage, into one cell
    STRING_AGG(
        CASE WHEN m.notes IS NOT NULL AND LEN(m.notes) > 0
             THEN m.stage + ': ' + m.notes
        END,
        ' | '
    ) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY m.stage)                                               AS [Discrepancy]
FROM metrics m
GROUP BY m.date_ingested, m.la_code;
GO


-- ---------- Report 2: File errors ------------------------------------------
CREATE OR ALTER VIEW dbo.vw_pipeline_file_errors
AS
SELECT
    e.error_message                          AS [error message],
    CONVERT(CHAR(8), e.date_ingested, 112)   AS [Date Ingestion],
    e.la_code                                AS [LA],
    e.file_name                              AS [File Name],
    e.file_format                            AS [Format],
    e.file_location                          AS [Location],
    e.comments                               AS [Comments]
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'pipeline_file_errors/',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'DataLakeAudit',
    FORMAT = 'DELTA'
) WITH (
    error_message VARCHAR(128),
    date_ingested DATE,
    la_code       VARCHAR(128),
    file_name     VARCHAR(512),
    file_format   VARCHAR(32),
    file_location VARCHAR(2000),
    comments      VARCHAR(1000)
) AS e;
GO


/* ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   SECTION C  --  How to query / filter
---------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

-- Latest day, all LAs, with discrepancies
SELECT *
FROM er_juror_audit.dbo.vw_pipeline_row_counts
WHERE [Date Ingested] = (SELECT MAX([Date Ingested]) FROM er_juror_audit.dbo.vw_pipeline_row_counts)
ORDER BY [LA];

-- Today's errors only
SELECT *
FROM er_juror_audit.dbo.vw_pipeline_file_errors
WHERE [Date Ingestion] = CONVERT(CHAR(8), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE), 112)
ORDER BY [LA], [File Name];
